data.title: Singapore Inflation Eased in January
data.description:
By Amanda Lee

SINGAPORE--Singapore's consumer inflation and the closely watched core measure both cooled in January, showing that price pressures are aligning with policymakers' aims.

The consumer price index rose 1.2% last month from a year ago, the Department of Statistics said Monday. That compared with December's revised 1.5% increase and the median estimate for a rise of 1.95% in a Wall Street Journal poll of six economists.

Core CPI, which strips out private road transport and accommodation costs, rose 0.8% in January, the data showed. That was slower than December's 1.8% growth and the 1.6% expansion expected of five economists in the WSJ poll.

The data came after the Monetary Authority of Singapore in January eased its monetary policy for the first time in five years. A continuous slowing in core inflation may back the case for the central bank to further ease its policy stance, which is centered on the city-state's exchange rate.

While the MAS doesn't have a set target for inflation, it views core inflation of just under 2% on average as consistent with overall price stability in the economy.

 
 
Sector                         Index Weighing         % Change YoY 
Housing & Utilities                    29.38%                 +1.4 
Food                                   20.42%                 +1.5 
Transport                              13.07%                 +3.1 
Recreation, Sport & Culture             5.95%                 -0.7 
Education                               5.79%                 +0.1 
Misc Goods & Services                   4.38%                 +0.2 
Information &Communication              3.81%                 -0.2

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

NZX 50 Sinks to October Levels
data.description:
New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index fell 1.7% to 12,532 on Monday, sinking for the fifth straight session to its lowest close since early October 2024, weighed by broad sector losses.

This followed a slump on Wall Street Friday, driven by US data raising concerns over a slowing economy and persistent inflation.

Adding to the negative sentiment, President Trump reportedly ordered restrictions on Chinese investments in key US sectors, escalating tensions and impacting New Zealand due to its trade ties with China.

Domestically, strong Q4 retail sales data, reinforced expectations of slower rate cuts, adding pressure on the index.

Utility, aged-care, and real estate stocks saw notable declines, with Mercury NZ, Contact Energy, and Meridian Energy losing 0.7% to 1.9%.

All of the 25 largest stocks by market cap ended lower.

However, Westpac, Heartland, and ANZ pared losses, rising 0.7% to 1.5%.

Year-to-date, the NZX 50 is now down 4.4%.

data.title: Singapore Core Inflation Lowest Since 2021
data.description:
Core consumer prices in Singapore climbed 0.8% year-on-year in January 2025, sharply easing from a 1.8% rise in the previous month and below market forecasts of 1.5%.

This marked the lowest core inflation rate since June 2021.

Last year, core inflation averaged 2.7%, much lower than 4.2% in 2023.

Core inflation is projected to fall further in 2025 as services prices have been on a moderating trend.

data.title: AD Ports' Noatum Maritime, Erkport Form New Shipping Services JV
data.description:
Abu Dhabi Ports (ADX:ADPORTS), d/b/a AD Ports, subsidiary Noatum Maritime, and Turkey's Erkport formed a joint venture to enhance vehicle logistics and roll-on/roll-off shipping services.

Under the agreement, the diversified maritime services provider will own a 60% stake in the JV, while Erkport will own the remaining 40% interest, according to a Monday release. The venture, operating under the brand name United Global Ro-Ro, will develop integrated regional feeder networks, minimize transit times, and enhance high and heavy cargo delivery for customers across Europe, the Mediterranean Sea, South Africa, the Arabian Gulf, and Asia.

Both companies will deploy 11 vessels including container roll-on/roll-off, pure car and truck carrier, and roll-on/roll-off vessels on five services.

data.title: Australia's corporate watchdog penalises two former Star executives on duty breach
data.description:
Australia's corporate regulator said on Monday it has fined and disqualified two former executives of Star Entertainment after they admitted to breaches of their duties, with the total fine amounting to A$240,000 ($153,096).

($1 = 1.5676 Australian dollars)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Monday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Warren Buffett Occasionally Writes to Shareholders About Overheated Markets. Not This Year.

The Berkshire Hathaway chairman acknowledged that "often, nothing looks compelling" in the hunt for equity investments.

Australia's Perpetual Ends Talks With KKR Over $1.39B Asset Sale

Australia's Perpetual ended talks with KKR about a potential asset sale to the U.S. buyout firm, and will instead look to hold onto its corporate trust unit.

The Long Nights and Drug Addiction That Drove a Banker to Insider Trading

John Femenia says he conceived of the scheme while high on stimulants and working late as an investment banker.

As banks retreat from DEI, Citi is making these changes to its diversity and hiring practices

Citigroup says it will no longer require "diverse slates of candidates and diverse panels of interviewers."

Coinbase Says SEC Intends to Drop Lawsuit Against Crypto Exchange

The agency had sought to oversee the company as a stock market.

Khosla Ventures Hits Fundraising Trail

Vinod Khosla's firm Khosla Ventures, a leading backer of artificial intelligence and other startups, has started reaching out to potential investors for its next set of funds, according to people familiar with the situation.

Crypto exchange Bybit suffers $1.4 billion hack. Here's why it's troubling for the industry.

The hack of Bybit is the latest incident in which the crypto industry has been targeted.

Why Is Warren Buffett Hoarding So Much Cash?

Investors are poised to study the Berkshire Hathaway chairman's annual letter for insights about the stock market.

The Latest Dilemma Facing Finance Chiefs: What to Tell Investors About Tariffs

CFOs are weighing how much information to share about the impact of tariffs, especially as many are yet to take effect and the landscape continues to evolve.

Private Equity Sees Deal Pipelines Filling In as Year Begins

Private-equity executives entered 2025 with high hopes for a dealmaking recovery that is lifting payouts to fund investors and powering fundraising.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Monday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Oil prices end lower, erasing weekly gains

"A number of decisions are pending ... that could drive the oil price in one direction or the other," one analyst noted.

Trump Paralyzes the U.S. Wind Power Industry

The president, who despises wind turbines, has paused federal permits and leasing for such projects, putting company plans in limbo.

Africa's Richest Man and His $20 Billion Oil Refinery From Hell

After making billions in cement, Nigerian Aliko Dangote is tackling oil and corruption in his quest to become Africa's Rockefeller.

Fossil-Fuel Companies Are Lobbying Trump to Save a Green-Fuel Program

Oil-and-gas companies are pushing the new administration to keep hydrogen tax credits, despite the president's ambition to destroy Biden-era climate incentives.

Trump's Energy Plans Are Creating Surprising Opportunities

It's not all about oil, one of the president's top priorities. Why natural gas leads our list of the industry's most promising investments. Even some solar and wind stocks could climb.

Adnoc Sells $2.84 Billion of Shares in Gas Subsidiary

The move is to increase Adnoc Gas's free float and paves the way for the subsidiary's inclusion in share indexes.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories increased by more than expected last week in a fourth consecutive build, while product stocks declined, according to U.S. government data.

How Tariffs Could Shock America's Power System

Transformers used in power grids are especially vulnerable to trade disruptions.

Schneider Electric Beats Market Views

The energy-management and automation group posted higher net profit and revenue for 2024 on continued strength in energy management, beating market views.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Read about the U.S. rig count, Sembcorp Industries, Petronas Dagangan, and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

data.title: Italy - Factors to watch on Feb. 24
data.description:
The following factors could affect Italian markets on Monday.

Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy. New items are marked with (*).

For a complete list of diary events in Italy please click on .

DEBT

Treasury details of BTP and CCTeu bonds, with relative amounts to be auctioned on Feb 27.

Treasury said on Friday it would sell 4.0 bln euros in BOT bills at auction on Feb 26.

COMPANIES

SAIPEM

Italian oil services company is close to a merger with Norwegian rival Subsea 7 , Italian newspaper La Repubblica reported on Sunday without citing sources.

FERRARI

The total remuneration of the luxury carmaker CEO Benedetto Vigna rose to 7.98 million euros in 2024 from 6.69 million in 2023, a filing showed on Friday.

TELECOM ITALIA , POSTE ITALIANE

Poste Italiane is ready to support consolidationin Italy's hypercompetitive telecoms market after the national post office this month became a leading investor in Telecom Italia, CEO Matteo Del Fante said on Friday during an analyst call on Poste's results.

INWIT

The CEO of the newly-formed Fastweb-Vodafone group told on Sunday La Repubblica daily that he wanted to engage in a constructive dialog with Inwit, which he inherited from the British group, in order to create value for all.

DIARY

Rome, Italy-United Arab Emirates Business Dialogue Forum (0800 GMT); Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani and UAE Foreign Trade Minister Thani bin Ahmed Al Zeyoudi deliver introductory addresses; expected attendees include Industry Minister Adolfo Urso, UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan and Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Stock Futures Rise on Conservatives Win in German Elections
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

EU CPI; Germany ifo business sentiment; trading update from Sasol

Opening Call:

European stock futures gained; Asian equities are mixed; the dollar weakened; oil futures fell and gold rose

Equities:

European stock futures were tracking higher at the start of the week amid risk-on sentiment as investors reacted to the outcome of Germany's elections with the conservative Christian Democrat Union winning the most votes and looking to form a coalition government.

In the U.S., concerns about a weakening American consumer sent stocks lower last week.

This week's economic highlights include the release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures Price Index for January, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, on Friday.

Nvidia's fourth-quarter results on Wednesday are the big earnings to watch this week. BofA analysts said in a recent note that the results will mark the "next important test for AI bulls."

Forex:

The euro gained against other currencies in the early Asian session as traders digested the outcome of Germany's election.

"However, it is unclear whether two or three parties will be needed to form a coalition government," CBA's Global Economic & Markets Research team said.

Market participants will likely be closely watching whether the eventual government agrees to reform the 'debt brake' to support economic growth, the team added.

--

The U.S. dollar weakened amid prospects of Fed rate cuts as U.S. economic data released last Friday were disappointing, NAB said.

The weak U.S. data led to the overnight-index-swap market bringing forward a 25 bp rate cut by the Fed to July from September, Catril added.

Bonds:

With uncertainties mounting around Fed policy and tariffs, fixed income requires caution, said Dan Genter of Genter Capital.

He likes Treasurys with maturities up to seven years. "We are not going to push too much far out into the curve." Long-term bond prices, which fell as yields rose in recent months, might look attractive, but "it's too unclear, with regards to the direction of the tail of the curve," Genter said.

The uncertainty basically stems from a lack of clarity on how tariffs and other Trump policies will affect inflation. Genter expects the inflationary impact to be minimal, but until it becomes more clear to investors "there is no direction at all."

Energy:

Oil prices fell, likely weighed by prospects of more supply. The Trump administration is reportedly pressuring Iraq to resume Kurdish crude oil exports to Turkey, Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia said.

Also, recent data from the EIA reinforce oil prices' bearish trend, the research strategist said adding that the increase of 4.6 million barrels in U.S. crude inventories for the week ended Feb. 14 suggests weaker-than-expected domestic demand.

Metals:

Gold edged higher supported by Friday's weak U.S. economic data that may enhance the haven appeal of the precious metal.

Demand for gold is being driven mainly by Western investors and central banks, Commerzbank Research's Carsten Fritsch said. ETF investors also seem to be jumping on the bandwagon, the commodity analyst added.

--

Copper was lower but bullish factors may still dominate in the short term with prices supported by expectations of tight supply, said Nanhua Futures analysts.

However, in the medium term, potential downside risks may emerge on weaker demand due to tariff policies and gradual easing of supply constraints, the analysts added.

--

Iron ore fell in Asian trade. The ferrous metal's fundamentals appear to have strong support, Nanhua Futures analysts said.

On the valuation side, market sentiment has recovered after recent data showed strong steel consumption in China. Investors are also awaiting China's National People's Congress meeting in March for any additional property-supportive measures.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

The U.S. Economy Depends More Than Ever on Rich People

Many Americans are pinching pennies, exhausted by high prices and stubborn inflation. The well-off are spending with abandon.

The top 10% of earners-households making about $250,000 a year or more-are splurging on everything from vacations to designer handbags, buoyed by big gains in stocks, real estate and other assets.

What Do Mass Federal Layoffs Mean for the Labor Market?

America's biggest employer, the federal government, is laying off workers in droves. Depending on how many, and which, jobs get cut, the blow to the U.S. job market could be merely glancing, or seriously damaging.

As of January, the U.S. government had 2.4 million civilian employees, excluding the postal service. That is a lot of people, though just 1.5% of total nonfarm jobs. So what will it mean for the economy if hundreds of thousands of those jobs go away?

The 'next important test for AI bulls' happens this week with Nvidia's earnings

Shares of Nvidia Corp., the chip-making giant at the core of the artificial-intelligence boom, have recovered - somewhat - from the hit they took during the market's DeepSeek tantrum last month. But as the $3.4 trillion company prepares to report fourth-quarter results on Wednesday, Wall Street has other anxieties.

For one, as MarketWatch noted last week, the results will offer a closer look at its new Blackwell chip architecture. Production has ramped up, but the products have faced concerns over shortages, delays and issues with overheating that have reportedly prompted big customers put off orders.

Ukraine's Zelensky Wants Better Terms on Minerals Deal Demanded by Trump

KYIV, Ukraine-The U.S. and Ukraine pushed divergent messages over the weekend, with Kyiv pressing for better terms to the mineral-rights deal sought by President Trump, while Washington urged Ukraine to swiftly accept the proposal already on the table.

The dispute over the minerals proposal is part of mounting disagreements between the U.S. and its allies about how best to end the war in Ukraine. The leaders of France and the U.K. were set to head to Washington this week to lobby on behalf of Ukraine, as the Trump administration's position continues to stretch trans-Atlantic tensions to their greatest in years.

Conservative Friedrich Merz Is Poised to Win German Election

BERLIN-Friedrich Merz is the clear winner of the German election. The question now for the conservative leader is how fast and with whom he can cobble together a government-and whether the U.S. will seek to influence the process.

Despite a historically strong showing by antiestablishment nationalists in a ballot that extended Europe's recent lurch to the right, early estimates indicated that Merz's Christian Democratic Union was on the way to a comfortable victory.

Tesla, Uber Head Toward a Showdown in Austin

The battle over self-driving cars and the future of ride-hailing is slated to start in Austin, Texas, in just a few weeks.

How it goes can help investors figure out how trillions of dollars in market value will be allocated.

How Porsche, Volvo and Rivian Are Injecting Unique Driving Styles Into Self-Driving Cars

A Porsche programmed with aggressive, sporty moves; a Volvo tuned to a more cautious pace; an autonomous Rivian that takes the scenic route, on-road or off.

In the future, cars won't only drive autonomously: Using software and artificial intelligence, their makers will give them distinct driving styles.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Singapore core inflation at 0.8% y/y, lower than forecast
data.description:
Singapore's key consumer price gauge rose 0.8% in January from a year earlier, lower than economist forecasts, official data showed on Monday.

The core inflation rate, which excludes private road transport and accommodation costs, compared with a 1.5% forecast by a Reuters poll of economists.

Headline inflation was 1.2% in annual terms in January, lower than economists' forecast of 2.15%.

Statistics Singapore said the CPI had been rebased to a base year of 2024 from 2019. The annual change in the headline inflation rate in December was revised to 1.5% from 1.6%

data.title: Sinomach Heavy Equipment Signs Agreement for Cambodia Hydropower Project
data.description:
Sinomach Heavy Equipment Group signed key agreements for a $996 million pumped-storage hydropower project in Cambodia's Tatai River Basin.

The project aims to support Cambodia's renewable energy transition and improve power grid stability, according to a Feb. 22 filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The agreements, signed with the Cambodian Ministry of Mines and Energy and Electricité du Cambodge, cover project execution, power purchase and land leasing.

The 1,000-megawatt facility will operate for 40 years after a five-year construction period.

data.title: BMW to Reevaluate Schedule of Electric Cars Production in UK
data.description:
BMW will review the schedule for producing the fully electric Mini model in the UK, Reuters reported Feb. 22.

Citing uncertainties surrounding the automotive industry, the German luxury vehicle manufacturer said it is rethinking the original plan of starting the model's production in the UK in 2026. It allocated 600 million pounds sterling for the plan to transform its Mini brand into an all-electric model by 2030.

Amid the review, construction continues at the production site "to make the plant future-ready," Mini noted, adding that it had notified the UK government of the plan to review the production schedule.

data.title: Market Chatter: Bank of Korea Likely to Cut Key Rate to 2.75%
data.description:
South Korea's central bank is expected to lower its policy rate by 0.25 percentage points this week, with 20 out of 21 analysts polled by Yonhap Infomax predicting a rate cut to 2.75%, Yonhap News reported Friday.

The central bank will release a revised growth forecast on Tuesday, Feb. 25.

Most experts anticipate a further reduction to 2.5% in the first half of the year. The Bank of Korea is also set to downgrade its growth forecast, with Governor Rhee Chang-yong hinting at a cut to around 1.6% from 1.9%, the report said.

If realized, this would mark the first time the country's annual growth rate declines below its 2% potential growth rate, it said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Oil prices slip as Kurdistan export resumption looms
data.description:
Oil prices slipped in Asia on Monday, extending losses from last week, on the prospect of a resumption of exports from Kurdistan's oilfields, while investors awaited clarity on talks to resolve Russia's war on Ukraine.

Brent futures were down 14 cents, or 0.2%, at $74.29 barrel, as of 0441 GMT, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures lost 22 cents, or 0.3%, to $70.18 a barrel.

Both Brent and WTI dropped by more than $2 on Friday, posting weekly declines of 0.4% and 0.5%, respectively.

"The downward spiral in crude oil prices is driven by pressure from the U.S. president on Iraq to resume oil exports from Kurdistan oilfields, which could improve supply flows in global oil markets after nearly two years of disruption," said Sugandha Sachdeva, founder of New Delhi-based research firm SS WealthStreet.

Iraq will export 185,000 barrels per day from Kurdistan's oilfields through the Iraq-Turkey pipeline once oil shipments resume, an Iraqi oil ministry official said on Sunday.

Iraq's oil ministry said all procedures had been completed to allow the resumption of exports through the Iraq-Turkey pipeline, potentially resolving a dispute that has disrupted crude flows.

All eyes remain on the progress of talks to end Russia's war on Ukraine, which enters its fourth year on Monday. Officials said on Sunday that European Union leaders will meet for an extraordinary summit on March 6 to discuss additional support for Ukraine and European security guarantees.

This comes after U.S. President Donald Trump initiated talks with Russia on ending the war but without inviting Ukraine or the European Union to the table. A senior Russian diplomat said Russian and U.S. teams plan to meet this week to discuss improving relations.

Sanctions by the U.S. and EU on Russian oil exports have curbed its shipments and disrupted seaborne oil supply flows. Global energy supplies are expected to increase if a peace deal is reached and sanctions are lifted.

Oil prices will be influenced by geopolitical developments and U.S. policy announcements in the short term, Sachdeva said.

In the Middle East, a Hamas official said talks with Israel through mediators on further steps in a ceasefire agreement are conditional on Palestinian prisoners being released as agreed.

Israel and Hamas have frequently accused each other of violations since the ceasefire started on January 19, but so far it has continued to hold.

data.title: SKALE to Participate in Emerging Tech Conference in Lincoln on March 7th
data.description:
SKALE co-founder Jack O’Holleran will speak at the Emerging Tech Conference on March 7th from 15:30 to 16:30 UTC in Linkoln. O’Holleran, an alumnus of the University of Nebraska–Lincoln, will address navigating risks in emerging technology. His return to his alma mater underscores his ongoing engagement with the academic community.

SKL Info

SKALE is a high-throughput, gas-free network of interconnected, modular blockchains. Designed to enhance Ethereum projects, it provides a scalable environment for developers to shift their projects from Ethereum, achieving better performance.

Operating in tandem with Ethereum, SKALE leverages its unique consensus mechanism. It effectively marries Ethereum’s proof-of-stake (PoS) framework with SKALE’s pooled security model. This synergy results in secure, rapid transactions devoid of gas fees, positioning SKALE as an efficient platform, particularly for Web3 and gaming applications, enhancing Ethereum’s scalability for wider adoption.

SKL, the utility token of SKALE, is an ERC-777 token, maintaining backward compatibility with the ERC-20 standard. Offering token-level delegation, it enables non-custodial staking directly from user wallets, bypassing traditional smart contract locks. Within the network, SKL finds its use in payments, especially for SKALE chain subscriptions. Token holders, be it as validators or delegators, can stake SKL to earn rewards. Validators, by running nodes, ensure the network's security, transaction validation, and smart contract execution, earning SKL in return. Delegators, while playing a passive role, earn a fraction of these rewards.

data.title: Aurora to Participate in Modular DeFi Research Day in Denver on February 25th
data.description:
Aurora will be featured at the Modular DeFi Research Day, taking place in Denver on February 25.

The co-founder, Alex Shevchenko is scheduled to present “Intent-Pilled DefAI” from 22:50 to 23:10 UTC, covering topics such as Bitcoin, scaling, NEAR Intents, and more.

Refer to the official tweet by AURORA:

Aurora
@auroraisnear

Feb 25th, 10:00 (MST)

Join @AlexAuroraDev at Modular DeFi Research Day by @encodeclub to hear the latest in Bitcoin, scaling, NEAR Intents and more.

Talk: "Intent-Pilled DefAI" | 15:50 - 16:10https://t.co/TD6nsaIrzp

Feb 20, 2025

AURORA Info

Aurora is a developer-friendly platform built on the NEAR protocol, providing compatibility with Ethereum. Aurora allows developers to easily port their smart contracts from Ethereum to NEAR, leveraging the benefits of NEAR's impressive block time, 2-second finality, and transaction fees of less than 1 cent due to the cost of gas.

Its primary objective is to facilitate the scalability of the Ethereum ecosystem, allowing developers to run their applications on a high-throughput, Ethereum-compatible, future-proof platform with low transaction costs for users.

Another important aspect of Aurora's goal is to increase the level of decentralization in the blockchain ecosystem. This is achieved through AuroraDAO, where holders of AURORA governance tokens can participate in decisions regarding the management and development of Aurora.

data.title: IoTeX to Participate in ETHDenver in Denver on February 26th
data.description:
IoTeX will participate in the upcoming ETHDenver conference, hosting an event titled “DePIN AI” on February 26th in Denver.

The event aims to explore the intersection of Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Networks (DePIN) and Artificial Intelligence, offering insights into real-world applications.

ETHDenver

February 26

Request to join nowhttps://t.co/yfvNIc0ltO

Feb 20, 2025

IOTX Info

IoTeX (IOTX) is an open-source blockchain platform specifically designed for the Internet of Things (IoT) with a strong emphasis on scalability, privacy, isolation, and the ability to develop decentralized applications and IoT ecosystems.

IoTeX was created to address the technical challenges faced by existing IoT ecosystems, such as security issues, high maintenance costs, and scalability limitations. The project is developing a new blockchain architecture to support a large number of IoT devices.

At the core of IoTeX is the concept of "blockchain within a blockchain." The main network, or "root blockchain," is responsible for network management and security, while internal blockchains, or "sub-blockchains," are used to manage various IoT devices and process transactions.

The IoTeX network utilizes its native token, IOTX, for operations such as transaction fee payments and participation in network governance.

data.title: SKALE to Host Webinar on February 25th
data.description:
SKALE will hold a webinar on February 25th at 15:00 UTC to discuss how Unity 6 and SKALE can enhance game development. The event will focus on the integration of Web3 technologies to provide seamless and gas-free gaming experiences.

Refer to the official tweet by SKL:

SKALE
@SkaleNetwork

Calling all game devs! 🧑â€💻

On Feb 25 at 7:00 AM PST, discover how @Unity 6 and SKALE can take your game to the next level.

Built for Web3, completely seamless, and entirely gas-free 🤙 pic.twitter.com/mqln3pwNZ7

Feb 20, 2025

SKL Info

SKALE is a high-throughput, gas-free network of interconnected, modular blockchains. Designed to enhance Ethereum projects, it provides a scalable environment for developers to shift their projects from Ethereum, achieving better performance.

Operating in tandem with Ethereum, SKALE leverages its unique consensus mechanism. It effectively marries Ethereum’s proof-of-stake (PoS) framework with SKALE’s pooled security model. This synergy results in secure, rapid transactions devoid of gas fees, positioning SKALE as an efficient platform, particularly for Web3 and gaming applications, enhancing Ethereum’s scalability for wider adoption.

SKL, the utility token of SKALE, is an ERC-777 token, maintaining backward compatibility with the ERC-20 standard. Offering token-level delegation, it enables non-custodial staking directly from user wallets, bypassing traditional smart contract locks. Within the network, SKL finds its use in payments, especially for SKALE chain subscriptions. Token holders, be it as validators or delegators, can stake SKL to earn rewards. Validators, by running nodes, ensure the network's security, transaction validation, and smart contract execution, earning SKL in return. Delegators, while playing a passive role, earn a fraction of these rewards.
